programme.title: Speed Racer [Japanese-Language Version]

programme.title: Speed Racer [Japanese-Language Version]

programme.title: Speed Racer [Japanese-Language Version]
programme.desc: By air, Go and his fellows are on the way to the Grand Prix, but their plane breaks down due to lightning, falling onto an uninhabited island with other passengers.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon decides to change her buying strategy, going for more of Philip Serrell's rust-and-woodworm fare. Phil diverts to the beach and asks a local fisherman if he has some antique chandlery to sell!

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon heads to Porthcurno to see the incredible cables that stretch from this little village to South America, Australia, and India. Phil Serrell goes for a ride on a replica of the Puffing Devil.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon heads to the Royal Mint and finds out how Sir Isaac Newton revolutionized coins to prevent forgers. Phil Serrell gets a lesson in the culture at the Bristol Old Vic and discovers why it's a miracle the building still stands.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw and Claire Rawle are in Northumberland. Paul discovers the Morpeth Olympics and Claire learns about the female Indiana Jones, Gertrude Bell. Everything's rosy until Claire mentions she loves militaria -- Paul's specialty!

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Claire Rawle finds out about the Tynemouth Volunteer Life Brigade and Paul Laidlaw visits a military observation post in Blyth. And Paul finds some RAF stereoscopic aerial reconnaissance photographs of German U-boats at a great price.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Claire Rawle discovers the history of big game fishing in the 1930s, while Paul Laidlaw sets off to rendezvous in a secret WWII bunker. Paul buys a pair of Charles Barlow vases, and Claire picks up an early 20th-century spectacle case.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw finds a rare 18th-century puzzle jug, and Claire Rawle some Shelley porcelain. Paul discovers how Hull was vital to thousands of Jews fleeing from Tsarist Russia and Claire indulges her passion for all things railway.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: In the final, Paul Laidlaw hopes a late 17th-century hand-forged halberd head will earn him a huge profit. Claire Rawle buys a Chinese cloisonné vase and an Edwardian inkstand. Will Claire be the comeback kid and crush the mighty Laidlaw?

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey take their classic car to an auction in Merseyside. Christina detours to learn about the history of the British seaside in Bognor Regis and Mark visits the last surviving Victorian Brickworks in Britain.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey's antiquing adventure continues in the Cotswolds. Mark discovers a rural idyll that inspired a best seller novel while Christina learns about an incredible war hero.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: In Cheshire, Christina Trevanion ends up deep underground and Mark Stacey finds himself in such a predicament he has to call for help! Mark finds a Home Guard practice rifle while Christina makes a peculiar purchase of a human jaw.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey travel in the West Midlands in their classic car. Mark visits the factory that profited from the Victorian obsession with death. Christina learns why the original Land Rover became a global phenomenon.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: It's a race to the finish! Just twenty pounds separates Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey. Mark is literally stuck and struggling waist-deep in antiques. Christina moves into property and buys a rather unique home.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin and Paul Laidlaw visit the West Coast of Scotland. Military buff Paul gets excited when he finds some desk ink stamps dating from the Cold War. Natasha ends up on ice as she gets to grips with an ancient sport in Ayrshire.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel's experts visit Mount Stewart in County Down, Northern Ireland, where they assess a badly damaged painting valued between £20 and £30,000, a souvenir from Laurel and Hardy, and Henry Sandon is introduced to a mug bearing his spitting image.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team returns to Mount Stewart in Northern Ireland, where their finds include a silver tea set presented to mark an officer's bravery at the battle of Trafalgar, a rare Wedgwood cup and saucer, and a teddy bear that has been in a box for 40 years.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the experts are in England, Woburn Abbey in Bedfordshire. Treasured items brought to light include a rare washing bowl for beards, a portrait of a handsome cat valued at 18,000 pounds, and a Victorian bracelet that imitates a snake.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A Victorian multi-purpose penknife, a miniature theatre, and a Japanese lacquered chest of astonishing rarity and considerable value are among the treasures discovered on a visit to Clitheroe in Lancashire.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel presents from Scarborough in North Yorkshire, where the artifacts include a rare surviving roll of Beatles wallpaper, the passport once belonging to the world's smallest man, and examples of some of the finest carved whales teeth.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the roadshow team are in St Ives, Cambridgeshire. They assess an early pocket calculator, a valuable jug bought for a fiver, and a traction engine arrives in full steam.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael's experts visit Haslar in Gosport and assess a satinwood secretaire (aka Aunt Selina) a radiograph of Edward VIII's hand, an Edward Lear sketch, a gold-painted bronze, a Steiff teddy, and ceremonial silverware given by Tsar Nicolas II to HMS Lion.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael's experts visit Wigan in Greater Manchester. Amongst the featured items are an Art Nouveau cabinet, a cache of silver buried in World War Two, and an early depiction of women's rights.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and his team of experts visit the London to Brighton Veteran Car Run, where they invite members of the public to bring along their prized possessions for expert examination.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the team visit the RHS gardens at Wisley. Amongst the pieces under scrutiny are an early fortune teller's caravan and a credenze with a potential value of £100,000.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel takes the team of experts to the Royal Horticultural Society gardens at Wisley. The treasures coming to light are a set of letters written by Lewis Carroll and a valuable carriage clock saved from a skip.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the team head for the village of Portmeirion in North Wales. Amongst the treasures brought to light are intricate 18th Century mosaics, items made by prisoners of war as gifts of thanks to local people, and a penknife valued at £1,500.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team head for Haltwhistle in Northumberland. Among the discoveries made are original fittings from the Titanic's sister ship, the Olympic, a Roman coin from Hadrians Wall, and a letter from JRR Tolkein.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: In Hastings, the team assesses a Meissen pot valued at £10,000, a sketch by artist Augustus John valued at between £10-15,000 and an unopened wedding gift from the 1920s. They also meet a man with a serious addiction to Punch and Judy.

programme.desc: Andrew is avoiding calls from his family in Liverpool. When he finally gives into Erica's demands and calls them, he receives some news that draws him back to his roots.

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Andrew arrives back in Liverpool and is meeting fierce objection from his mother over his treatment of her. Erica is involved in a highly sensitive child abuse case that splits Cardale in half.

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Erica arrives in Liverpool ready to spend the weekend with Andrew and his family, but she has her work cut out when the Attwood family seems intent on making things hard for her.

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Back from Liverpool, Andrew is ready to look to his future with Erica, but questions arise over her commitment. Meanwhile, David is concerned when Claire's behaviour begins to spiral out of control.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Pauline's gambling addiction finally comes to a head after an explosive confrontation in the surgery.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: A mysterious respiratory infection breaks out in the village.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: On the day of their wedding, Louisa's bridesmaid is hit in the eye with a party popper. Meanwhile, the local florist gets arrested and his shop is shuttered.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Life in the beautiful Cornish village of Portwenn has become ever more infuriating for Dr. Martin Ellingham since his and Louisa's heart-wrenching decision to call off their wedding.

programme.title: Hamish MacBeth
programme.desc: Police constable Hamish Macbeth is baffled by two apparently unrelated mysteries: a huge quantity of table salt has been stolen from Rory Campbell's general store; and Geordie Robb, a local bully, is missing.

programme.title: Hamish MacBeth
programme.desc: Newcomers to Lochdubh Vicky and Paul Jeffreys cause a stir: Vicky tries to put reluctant villagers back in touch with their Highland heritage, gradually alienating them.

programme.title: Hamish MacBeth
programme.desc: Major Maclean stages a robbery to make a false insurance claim. Before Hamish can settle things quietly, Inspector Bruce instigates a major surveillance operation.

programme.title: NCIS: Criminología Naval
programme.desc: Cuando un capitán de la Marina es encontrado muerto con un disfraz inusual debajo de su uniforme, NCIS descubre una sociedad secreta de superhéroes reales y debe rastrear al villano.

programme.title: NCIS: Criminología Naval
programme.desc: Un reservista naval de alta seguridad es encontrado muerto. Gibbs colabora con la Dra. Samantha Ryan de PsyOps para descubrir la verdad detrás del posible suicidio. Mientras tanto, DiNozzo discute con contabilidad tras ver el salario de McGee.

programme.title: Blue Bloods (en español): Culpable por asociación
programme.desc: Cuando un testigo clave en el caso de Danny es asesinado justo antes de testificar, Erin enfurece a Danny al pedirle ayuda a Anthony (Steve Schrippa) para encontrar al asesino.

programme.title: Blue Bloods (en español): Negocios personales
programme.desc: Después de que Emily Harrison (Danielle Savre) le dice a Danny y Baez que teme que su ex novio abusivo vuelva a comportarse de la misma manera con su nueva novia, Emily se convierte en la principal sospechosa cuando él es asesinado.

programme.title: Blue Bloods (en español): Confesiones
programme.desc: Frank se queda con poca información para encontrar a un niño desaparecido cuando un sacerdote conflictuado que podría ayudar se niega a romper el sello de confesión.

programme.title: Blue Bloods (en español): Pérdida irreparable
programme.desc: Cuando el hijo de Frank, el crítico más abierto de la comunidad afroamericana, el Reverendo Darnell Potter (Ato Essandoh), es asesinado, Potter debe dejar de lado su relación controvertida con la familia Reagan para obtener justicia para su hijo.

programme.title: Mentes criminales
programme.desc: Cuando se llama al UAC a Salt Lake City, Utah, para investigar un triple asesinato, la búsqueda de una conexión entre las víctimas podría llevarlos hasta un fugitivo que podría estar relacionado con el sospechoso.

programme.title: Mentes criminales
programme.desc: Al encontrar cadáveres congelados flotando en el lago en Nevada, la investigación de UAC los lleva a sospechar que las víctimas formaban parte de una secta que podría estar detrás de las muertes.

programme.title: Mentes criminales
programme.desc: La UAC está buscando a un asesino serial en Tallahasseeen, que llama a las autoridades para informar de los crímenes antes de cometer un asesinato.

programme.title: Mentes criminales
programme.desc: La UAC regresa a sus orígenes cuando miran uno de los viejos casos de Rossi y Gideon para localizar a un asesino que se escapó.

programme.title: NCIS: Criminología Naval
programme.desc: El equipo de NCIS investiga el asesinato de un teniente comandante de la Armada mientras lidia con el esposo devastado. Mientras tanto, Ziva reevalúa su futuro cuando regresa Ray.

programme.title: NCIS: Criminología Naval
programme.desc: Gibbs se enfrenta a un arma durante una parada rutinaria para tomar café, lo que lo obliga a cuestionar decisiones pasadas y presentes.

programme.title: NCIS: Criminología Naval
programme.desc: Cuando un capitán de la Marina es encontrado muerto con un disfraz inusual debajo de su uniforme, NCIS descubre una sociedad secreta de superhéroes reales y debe rastrear al villano.

programme.title: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders: Making the Team
programme.desc: The recruits do media training with NBC 5 Dallas-Fort Worth lead news anchors Meredith Land, Kelli, and Judy name two triangle points, and guest judge Melissa Rycroft sits in.

programme.title: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders: Making the Team
programme.desc: The ladies put their modeling skills to the test on photoshoot day, Julia tries to bounce back after her car accident, and the dancers get a lesson in healthy cooking.

programme.title: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders: Making the Team
programme.desc: With one week until the start of the season, Kelli and Judy make a tough cut, the show group performs for the first time, and Charlotte Jones Anderson judges rehearsal.

programme.title: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders: Making the Team
programme.desc: To make their final cut, Kelli and her team try to find one hopeful of the remaining 37 who isn't ready to perform at the Cowboys' home opener.

programme.title: Music City
programme.desc: Rachyl puts her dreams of becoming a lawyer on hold to support her husband, Kerry, in chasing after his goal of being a country musician in Nashville.

programme.title: Music City
programme.desc: When Kerry becomes unresponsive during his tour, the reality of his absence makes Rachyl question their future. Meanwhile, Jackson is ready to get closer to Jessica, but a surprise revelation may stand in the way of them getting more serious.

programme.title: Music City
programme.desc: The road calls and Kerry considers taking a gig on a tour that conflicts with his wedding anniversary. When Rachyl informs Kerry that she is seriously considering law schools in other states, Kerry puts his foot down.

programme.title: Music City
programme.desc: Jackson tries to take things slow with Jessica until unexpected temptations drive him back to his old ways. When Rachyl receives a text from an ex, her past threatens to derail her present. Alexandra competes in the Miss Tennessee USA Pageant.

programme.title: Music City
programme.desc: Jackson has to decide if he's ready for a serious relationship with Jessica, or if pageant girl Savana is more his speed. Rachyl struggles to tell Kerry about a recent text exchange with an Ex who just moved back to Nashville.

programme.title: Music City
programme.desc: Jackson might be biting off more than he can chew when he decides to perform live, and Kerry progresses his music career when he connects with another singer.

programme.title: Music City
programme.desc: After Kerry comes home late after a night out with Brooke, Rachyl grapples with the state of their relationship. Jackson practices with Jessica for his upcoming birthday performance, but Savana becomes suspicious of how much time they spend together.

programme.title: Music City
programme.desc: On the season finale, Rachyl and Kerry make a big decision about their future, and Jackson comes to a realization about Jessica (and his music) after Savana's ultimatum.

programme.title: Music City
programme.desc: Kerry wants to hire a publicist, Alexandra prepares for the Miss USA pageant, Jessica looks forward to her wedding, and Rachyl reconnects with an old friend.

programme.title: Music City
programme.desc: Alexandra forces Jackson to make a big decision, Alisa believes Bryant has a secret, and Rachyl gets closer to Jeremiah, which causes tension between her and Jessica.

programme.title: Music City
programme.desc: Alexandra gets revenge on Jackson for flaking, Kerry returns home to find Rachyl has been spending time with another guy, and Alisa confronts Bryant's photographer.

programme.title: Music City
programme.desc: Jackson buys a ring for Baylee despite his friends' concerns, Rachyl bonds with Alexandra after her fight with Jessica, and Bryant criticizes Alisa for lacking motivation.

programme.title: Music City
programme.desc: Jackson recovers from a serious car accident, Jessica considers not inviting Rachyl to her wedding, and Kerry continues to worry about Rachyl's relationship with Jeremiah.

programme.title: Music City
programme.desc: Kerry's publicist pressures him into doing a sexy photo shoot with a female model, Alisa takes a songwriting session with Luke Pell, and Jessica clears the air with Rachyl.

programme.title: Music City
programme.desc: Jessica's bachelorette party takes an unexpected turn, Alexandra makes things awkward for Jackson and Baylee, and Bryant controls the fate of Alisa's song.

programme.title: Music City
programme.desc: As everyone travels to Arkansas for Jessica and Dre's wedding, Alexandra spills a secret, Alisa gets a surprise, Jackson becomes emotional, and Kerry learns the truth.

programme.title: Music City
programme.desc: Jessica and Dre's wedding day arrives, Rachyl faces repercussions after Kerry learns the truth, and Baylee sees a different side of Jackson.

programme.title: Music City
programme.desc: Rachyl and Kerry reach a decision about their marriage, Bryant makes a confession, Jessica and Dre enjoy married life, and Jackson and Baylee discuss their future together.

programme.desc: Lil Bit and Daddy's relationship divides the house, Mattie helps Murray score, the gang plans a pool party for Mattie's birthday, and Lil Bit admits the truth.

programme.desc: Dr. Bender has good news concerning Lise's condition, but Hervé is still worried about Mireille and Anna; the girls are introduced to their next target: a seemingly normal businessman.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos: El caso musical
programme.desc: En 1999, una niña de 12 años fue encontrada asesinada en su casa y cambió la sensación de seguridad que tenían los residentes de la pequeña ciudad de Waseca, Minnesota.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos: Pistas reveladoras
programme.desc: La desaparición de la atleta universitaria de Filadelfia, Aimee Willard, es investigada después de que su carro fuera encontrado al costado de la carretera.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: Bruce Miller fue asesinado a tiros en la oficina del negocio de depósito de chatarra que poseía. La escena del crimen estaba casi inmaculada.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: En la noche de Halloween de 2004, Adriane Insogna y Leslie Mazzara fueron brutalmente asesinadas en su casa de Napa, California.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: En este caso internacional de extorsión, asesinato e identidades robadas, el estafador Albert J. Walker asume la identidad del compañero de trabajo, Ronald Platt. Platt luego aparece muerto en un océano con un ancla atada alrededor de su torso.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: Cuando una estudiante universitaria desaparece, sus compañeros de clase estaban preocupados por la seguridad de ella y la de ellos.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: Una bailarina de gogó le dijo a su familia que necesitaba alejarse por un tiempo, y luego simplemente desapareció. Los investigadores sospecharon, porque ella no se llevó ni su auto ni su ropa.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: Mientras estaba practicando tiros en un hermoso día de primavera, Martin Dillon es asesinado con disparos. Su amigo sostiene que fue un extraño accidente, pero se necesitan más de 20 años para que la verdad salga a la luz.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: Un cazador de Colorado que había recibido tres disparos fue víctima de un crimen bien planeado. Los investigadores sospecharon dos armas y dos asesinos.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos: Ahogamiento seco
programme.desc: Cuando Genell Plude es encontrada muerta en su baño, la escena apunta a un suicidio.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: Los residentes de Noel, Missouri, se sorprendieron al enterarse de que su banco había sido asaltado y el presidente del banco había desaparecido.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: Parece que una mujer ha perdido el control de su camioneta SUV y ha caído en el barranco de un arroyo poco profundo.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: En el 2007, Shamaia Smith desaparece de un club de striptease en East Hartford, Connecticut. La policía considera varios patrones del club, pero se enfocan en un comerciante local, Kenneth Otto.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: Una niña de seis años corrió y se escondió cuando vio que su abuela era golpeada hasta causarle la muerte, pero el hombre la siguió, la golpeó y la agredió. Ella dijo que el agresor era su tío, quien fue condenado. Se retractó de su testimonio años después.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: Los doctores del hospital no pudieron determinar la causa de las alucinaciones y el intenso dolor de Bobby Curley. Lo trataron como un trastorno neurológico pero su estado se deterioró. Él estaba siendo envenenado.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos: El mal pie
programme.desc: La policía estaba desconcertada por una huella oscura encontrada en la escena del crimen donde un hombre había sido asesinado y dos adolescentes resultaron gravemente heridas.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: En 2000, Kristine Fitzhugh no se presentó a su clase de música y su esposo Ken la encontró muerta al pie de las escaleras de su casa de Palo Alto. Tras una investigación adicional, el luminol revela evidencia de un asesinato en la cocina.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: Una joven pareja decidió celebrar su primer aniversario de bodas con un viaje de campamento a Mount Hood.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: Una hermosa joven cayó a su muerte desde un acantilado con vistas al océano. Inicialmente, los investigadores pensaron que se trataba de un trágico accidente.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: ¿Cómo pudo el acosador obtener el código del sistema de seguridad del hogar de la víctima? ¿Cómo pudo robar sus fotografías personales? La policía necesitaba respuestas y las encontraron en el lugar más extraño.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: En 1980, Shannon Mohr, residente de Michigan, murió en lo que su nuevo esposo, David Davis, reportó que fue un accidente al montar a caballo. Después de su muerte, la familia de Shannon informó que Davis parecía estar lejos de estar agobiado por el dolor.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos: Fantasma en la máquina
programme.desc: Se investiga la aparente sobredosis y suicidio de la esposa del reverendo Bill Guthrie, Sharon, y los detectives recuperan archivos incriminatorios de la computadora de Bill y se enteran de su infidelidad.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: El sospechoso principal tenía un historial criminal y su licencia de conducir se encontró en la escena del crimen de un brutal homicidio doble.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos: Acechado en la noche
programme.desc: Cuando una víctima es brutalmente asesinada en su propia cama, los investigadores encuentran una huella de zapato en el barro de afuera, evidencia física que esperan que conduzca al asesino.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: Se encontró el cuerpo de una mujer joven y una pequeña fibra en la ropa de la víctima llevó a la policía de San Diego directamente al asesino más improbable.

programme.title: Crímenes imperfectos
programme.desc: En 1988, se descubrió el cuerpo de una mujer joven en un río de Ohio. La mayor parte de la evidencia había sido borrada, pero el hijo de 6 años de la víctima, sin saberlo, les dijo a los investigadores todo lo que necesitaban saber.

programme.title: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders: Making the Team

programme.title: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders: Making the Team

programme.title: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders: Making the Team

programme.title: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders: Making the Team

programme.title: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders: Making the Team

programme.title: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders: Making the Team

programme.title: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders: Making the Team
programme.desc: Talent and looks alone won't get you on the squad. Dreams are made and hopes are crushed as only 46 out of 59 candidates make it into Training Camp.

programme.title: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders: Making the Team

programme.title: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders: Making the Team

programme.desc: Focusing more on her music than Miles, Maya lets Zoë talk her into making a sexy music video.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Miles helps Maya discover who is behind her video going viral. Maya's hellbent on revenge, getting her into hot water.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Student council prez Drew is psyched about his Degrassi TV interview but when the story sheds a negative light, Drew’s stunned.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Drew is excited to have Bianca back in town for Thanksgiving but is surprised when she isn't as forthcoming as he expected.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: It’s Alli’s birthday, but all she can think about is Leo being sent back to France.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Alli is still lying to protect Leo, but her stories are beginning to unravel. Maya shows her rubber room classmates that she isn’t a princess.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Drew attempts juggling his new job and his new girl. To forget about Leo, Alli focusses on her science experiment. After getting thrown out of class for forgetting to wear a bra, Imogen plans a scathing piece for Degrassi TV.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Failing at balancing his life, Drew decides he has to break up with Zoë. Alli partners up with Dallas in an attempt to stay focused on her science experiment. Imogen takes her dress code fight online, but what does she really want to achieve?

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: A shocking discovery leads Miles to question his family life. Eli is slipping away, and Clare finds herself growing closer to Drew. Dallas wants to be with Alli, but the truth about her relationship with Leo could send him over the edge.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Miles is determined to out his father’s secrets, but he may not be ready for the whole truth.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: With rumours swirling about Zoë’s drunken adventures at a recent party, Becky files a DTV report about teen girls and alcohol.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Becky wants to put things right after making a false accusation about Zoë's assault. But once she discovers the truth and sees how deeply this is affecting everyone, Becky questions if she's doing the right thing.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Maya tries to help Zig out of a tough situation. Jenna sets her sights on winning the semi-formal king and queen crowns with Connor. Becky worries she will lose Imogen as a friend when new student Jack arrives.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Becky tries to get back in bestie rhythm with Imogen at the semi-formal, but is there more to their relationship than friendship?

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Dallas feels he's being racially profiled after he and Connor are held for questioning in connection with a terror incident.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Tristan confronts Mr. Yates about his harsh Drama class criticisms and finds out the shocking truth behind them.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: When Zoë lands her dream role, it hits a little too close to home following her assault. Clare has some tough decisions to make as she searches for a date to Wild Wild West Night. Miles focuses on settling the score with Zig.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Fireworks begin at Wild Wild West Night when Clare makes her decision known with regards to Drew and Eli. Zoë struggles to stand up for herself. Miles and Zig face off in a showdown for Maya's heart.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Maya is doing her best to keep Zig safe but when she snoops and finds a bag of drugs, her worst fears are confirmed. Winston is on the prowl for his first ladyfriend when he realizes the perfect girl has been right under his nose for years.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Hiding Zig out at the Matlin house while the Maya organizes the Art Night seems to be working… for now. But will she be able to keep the gang away? Clare is deparate for answers from Drew.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Becky feels isolated at home since helping turn in her brother. Looking for a distraction, she finds her escape in online gaming. Drew’s excited for the last days of the Hollingsworth campaign but his charm gets him more than he bargained for.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Drew is in trouble! He was fired from the campaign, his friends think he’s a home-wrecker, and he’s even in a fight with Dallas.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: When Tristan asks Mr. Yates for some writing help for his MC gig, Mr. Yates suggests he come to his apartment for some extra help. Imogen crushes hard on Jack and is determined to prove she can “roll” with her crowd.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Tristan is warned by Mr. Yates to make his feelings about their affair less obvious at school but Tristan can’t help it, he’s beaming.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Zoë's trial begins and when she fears that she is being painted in a harsh light, she takes matters into her own hands. Becky is torn up about what she’s going to say when she takes the stand as a character witness in her brother’s trial.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Rick decides to get back at his bullies, hitting them right where it hurts – their cars. But when Jimmy becomes a member of the “Whack Your Brain” team, the two form a truce.

programme.title: Walking with Beasts
programme.desc: Going back 25 million years to the time of the indricothere, which was the largest mammal ever to live on dry land, being seven metres high and weighing 15 tons! The only threat to an adult would have been the hyaenadon which was the size of a rhino.

programme.title: Walking with Beasts
programme.desc: The beginnings of mankind can be traced back 3.2 million years to Ethiopia and the advent of Australopithecus, a type of ape which, like humans, was bipedal. These early members of the human family faced many dangers, including the sabre-toothed cat.

programme.title: Walking with Beasts
programme.desc: Revealing the exotic, oversized creatures that inhabited South America one million years ago, such as the deadly smilodon, the largest of all sabre tooth cats.

programme.title: Walking with Beasts
programme.desc: Looking at the depths of the Ice Age, following a group of mammoths who have been forced to travel south with the advent of winter. On a dangerous journey across the Alps, many become trapped in bogs or fall victim to predators.

programme.title: Walking with Monsters: Life Before Dinosaurs
programme.desc: The evolution of the predators who lived 550 million years ago, and how the first amphibians migrated from sea to land.

programme.title: Walking with Monsters: Life Before Dinosaurs
programme.desc: Three hundred million years ago, the forests were ruled by insects. This was a world in which giant dragonflies battled with two-foot-wide spiders.

programme.title: Walking with Monsters: Life Before Dinosaurs
programme.desc: On the supercontinent Pangea, early therapsids, creatures more similar to mammals than reptiles, fight for survival alongside other animals. In the carboniferous swamps, insects and arthropods take over and grow into giants.

programme.title: Science of Walking With Beasts
programme.desc: This episode explains how the 120-million-year reign of the dinosaurs ended when mammals became the fastest and fiercest creatures on the planet.

programme.title: Science of Walking With Beasts
programme.desc: A look at the science that has proved Darwin's idea that intelligent, sophisticated humans have an ancestral connection with the ape.

programme.title: Walking with Dinosaurs
programme.desc: The life of the first reptilian carnivores on Earth, told through computer animation.

programme.title: Walking with Dinosaurs
programme.desc: The Jurassic period saw the advent of the largest and strongest dinosaurs that ever roamed the Earth.

programme.title: Walking with Dinosaurs
programme.desc: A look at the world as it was 149 million years ago - in the late Jurassic Period. The old continents have broken up and much of the land is under water. Huge shallow seas have supported a revolution in marine life.

programme.title: Walking with Dinosaurs
programme.desc: With wings longer than those of a small plane, pterosaurs were the largest creatures ever known to have flown.

programme.title: Walking with Dinosaurs
programme.desc: This programme looks at the Cretaceous period, 106 million years ago. Volcanic activity forces the continents apart, and dinosaurs diversify into a range of species to cope with environmental extremes.

programme.title: Walking with Dinosaurs
programme.desc: It is the late Cretaceous period and Tyrannosaurus rex is the top predator, but the Earth is poisoned by volcanic activity and a comet strikes.

programme.title: Land of Giants: A Walking With Dinosaurs Special
programme.desc: Witness the spectacle of the fearsome predator Giganotosaurus.

programme.title: The Giant Claw: A Walking With Dinosaurs Special
programme.desc: Zoologist Nigel Marvin travels back in time on a prehistoric safari.

programme.title: Walking with Dinosaurs
programme.desc: Scientists study the perfectly preserved remains of an adolescent allosaurus they call Big Al.

programme.title: Walking with Dinosaurs
programme.desc: This scientific detective story traces the evidence for Big Al the allosaur's existence, using clues from climate change studies, forensics, and fossil records to bring him back to life.

programme.title: Walking with Dinosaurs: Shortbites
programme.desc: Featured species are the coelophysis and the diplodocus.

programme.title: Walking with Dinosaurs: Shortbites
programme.desc: Featured species are the opthalmosaurus and the ornithocheirus.

programme.title: Walking with Dinosaurs: Shortbites
programme.desc: Featured species are the leaellynasaura and the tyrannosaurus.

programme.title: The Making Of: Walking With Dinosaurs
programme.desc: Animatronics, computer animation and science re-create the dinosaurs' world.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Inferno
programme.desc: UNIT is at a project run by Professor Stahlman who intends to penetrate Earth’s crust. But the drilling has already released something deadly from beneath the Earth’s surface.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Inferno
programme.desc: The green slime oozing from the drill head is transforming men into monsters. Stahlman refuses to listen to the warnings and resorts to sabotage so that his project may continue.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Inferno
programme.desc: Transported to a parallel world, the Doctor discovers that England is under military rule and the drilling project is at a more advanced stage.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Inferno
programme.desc: In the parallel world, the drilling project is about to penetrate the Earth’s crust. The Doctor, arrested as a spy, is unable to convince his captors of the danger that faces them.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Inferno
programme.desc: In the parallel world, Earth’s crust has been penetrated, releasing terrible forces from within the Earth. The Doctor must escape back to his world to prevent the same catastrophe.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Inferno
programme.desc: As the Earth in the parallel world faces destruction, the Doctor is desperate to return to his world to warn them of the danger. But time is running out.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Inferno
programme.desc: The Doctor has escaped the doomed, parallel Earth and has returned to his own world where he attempts to put a halt to the drilling project.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Terror of the Autons
programme.desc: The Master paves the way for an invasion by the insidious Nestene consciousness.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Terror of the Autons
programme.desc: The Master uses his hypnotic powers to take control of a factory where he creates Autons.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Terror of the Autons
programme.desc: The only clue to the Master's plans is a single plastic daffodil.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Terror of the Autons
programme.desc: The Master is broadcasting an activation signal to open a channel for the Nestenes.

programme.title: Doctor Who: The Mind of Evil
programme.desc: The Doctor and Jo arrive at Stangmoor Prison for a demonstration of the Keller Machine, a device capable of extracting negative emotions from criminal minds.

programme.title: Doctor Who: The Mind of Evil
programme.desc: A prison riot puts Jo in danger while the Doctor assists the Brigadier. Meanwhile, at the World Peace Conference, the Chinese delegate's aid prepares to kill the American delegate.

programme.title: Doctor Who: The Mind of Evil
programme.desc: Newly recovered, the Doctor escapes from his cell with Jo. The Master plans to use the Keller Machine to recruit the rioting inmates at Stangmoor Prison and attack the World Peace Conference.

programme.title: Doctor Who: The Mind of Evil
programme.desc: The mind parasite within the Keller Machine becomes too powerful for the Master to control. It learns how to move on its own and goes on the hunt for new minds.

programme.title: Doctor Who: The Mind of Evil
programme.desc: The Master forms an uneasy alliance with the Doctor so that between them they can subdue the Keller Machine which is now out of control.

programme.title: Doctor Who: The Mind of Evil
programme.desc: The Brigadier brings the prison under control, but the Master has escaped and plans to start a world war. The Keller Machine breaks free of the Doctor's restraining device and the Doctor has no choice but to turn the parasite against the Master.

programme.title: Doctor Who: The Daemons
programme.desc: The Doctor tries to prevent Professor Horner from breaking into an ancient barrow near the village of Devil’s End. Posing as the village vicar, the Master summons dark forces.

programme.title: Doctor Who: The Daemons
programme.desc: Devil’s End is sealed off from the outside world by a heat barrier. The Doctor and Jo return to the barrow where they are confronted by the gargoyle, Bok.

programme.title: Doctor Who: The Daemons
programme.desc: The Doctor attempts to help the Brigadier penetrate the heat barrier but is attacked by the Master’s servant in a helicopter. The Master prepares to summon the Daemon Azal.

programme.title: Doctor Who: The Daemons
programme.desc: The Doctor is captured by the villagers who are under the Master’s control. Jo goes to the church to prevent the Master from summoning Azal for the final time but is too late.

programme.title: Doctor Who: The Daemons
programme.desc: UNIT have penetrated the heat barrier and launch an attack on the gargoyle, Bok. In the cavern, the Daemon Azal confronts the Doctor and the Master to decide the fate of mankind.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Day of the Daleks
programme.desc: When Sir Reginald Styles claims to have seen a ghost, UNIT is called in. The Doctor and Jo spend the night at Styles' house and become the target of an assassin.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Day of the Daleks
programme.desc: As captive of the guerrillas, the Doctor discovers they intend to change history. Jo is transported to the 22nd Century where she unwittingly gives vital information to the Daleks.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Day of the Daleks
programme.desc: The Doctor arrives in a bleak future where Earth is ruled by the Daleks and mankind is enslaved. Rescuing Jo, the two escape but the brutish Ogrons are in pursuit.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Day of the Daleks
programme.desc: The Controller betrays the Daleks and is exterminated. The Daleks travel back in time to the 20th Century to ensure that the peace conference fails.

programme.title: Doctor Who: The Curse of Peladon
programme.desc: The planet Peladon is hoping to join the Galactic Federation. But King Peladon’s Chancellor has been murdered and it seems that the legendary royal beast, Aggedor is to blame.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: A firehouse Dalmatian; a dog becomes aggressive toward his owners after being attacked by two other dogs.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: A border collie/springer spaniel mix bolts toward small animals; a shepherd mix has a biting problem; a pushy poodle.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Basset hounds sibling rivalry; aggressive dog; possessive poodle-mix.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Cesar helps a disrespectful yellow Lab who causes chaos everywhere she goes and some dogs who are misbehaving at the office.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: A French bulldog fails the doggy day-care entrance exam; three Jack Russell terriers fight one another for their owner's attention; a beagle howls incessantly while on walks.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: A mail carrier learns to thwart dog attacks; two Pomeranians attack each other when left alone; a Swiss mountain dog is aggressive around food.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: A miniature schnauzer compulsively spins in circles; four French bulldogs do not listen to their owner; a mixed breed has a leash tied so tightly it has to be surgically removed.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Cesar Milan is called in to help singer Patti LaBelle who is afraid of her own guard dog and a teacher who wants to unify her dog's dual personalities.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: A ridgeback mix has eccentricities; a Jack Russell terrier attacks motorcycles; a border collie's herding instincts get the best of him.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: An Australian shepherd is too dedicated to its owner; a beagle mix lashes out at anyone who walks past; an Italian mastiff fears cars.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: A puppy terrorizes its owner's cat; a spoiled Yorkshire terrier; a Rottweiler is aggressive toward strangers.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: An Italian greyhound mix is possessive over bones and toys; a mastiff misbehaves outside; a Yorkshire terrier takes over her owner's morning routine.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: A rescued dog is too ferocious; an insane Great Dane upsets a household; playing matchmaker for a lonely but picky boxer.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Pets abandoned during Hurricane Katrina reunite with their owners or find new homes; a pit bull behaves aggressively toward the dog of his owner's new girlfriend; a large dog has pica.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: The hunt for an elusive and clever criminal takes Dog through the world of a bantam fighting cock, a pregnant daughter, and a jungle safe house go together. And don't forget the mopeds as well as a group of hostile squatters on government land!

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: What's a father to do? When the cops ask Dog for help tracking down a renegade teen with a history of running hard, Baby Lyssa, eight months pregnant and counting, insists on joins the hunt. Can Dog keep his daughter safe and catch his man?

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: For Dog and Beth, it's not only another day in paradise, it's their anniversary. Beth is hoping for a romantic dinner with her man, but she knows only too well that for Dog work always comes first.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Today Dog finds himself looking for more than one fugitive. He starts the day looking for a woman who has skipped out on her court date. But he soon learns that she has teamed up with a woman whose criminal exploits have attracted the local media.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog gave a break to a young man with a drug problem who promised to enter drug rehab. But this commitment to rehabilitation seems to have ended when he bolted from the car on the way to the drug treatment center and went on the run.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog's latest fugitive doesn't think small when it comes to illegal drugs: he was carrying almost a pound of meth when he was arrested. Now he's on the run with his pregnant girlfriend on the high plains of Colorado.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog is back in Hawaii and tracking a dangerous fugitive through the jungles of the Big Island. Their prey is being protected by a ring of family and friends that Dog must forcefully confront to bring in his man.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: When Dog decides to spend the day caring for his newest granddaughter, Beth enlists the girls to hunt down a female fugitive. Heading into a dangerous part of town, the ladies will use their feminine wiles to track this mother gone wrong.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog and his old friends, Tim Chapman and Sonny Westbrook are riding with the team, so their target, a thief with violent tendencies, is out of luck. When Dog learns that the fugitive is also a disrespectful son, he is more motivated to find his man.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog doesn't have the whole team today, and he's got to catch a fugitive with a record of criminal assault. But when Dog finds the fugitive's former girlfriend, she thinks he isn't dangerous at all.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: For months, this fugitive has been stringing Leland along with a series of barely believable excuses. Out of patience, Leland calls in the Big Dog. The team whole team heads out to put pressure on the family and friends of the fugitive.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog's latest quarry is running from abuse and assault charges. As Dog investigates, he discovers that this fugitive has gamed the system by jumping from one bond, and one bondsman, to another.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: When the co-signer of a bond is also romantically involved with the defendant, sometimes the bondman is put in the middle of a lover's quarrel.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog is in Denver helping Bobby Brown to track down a fugitive with a long rap sheet. But, unfortunately, they have no picture of the felon to work with, so the challenges are big on this one. But, Dog discovers a new source; "Facebook".

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Back in Colorado, Dog's on the hunt for a runner with more than a decade's worth of trouble with the law. But the fugitive's friends and family are reluctant to help Dog bring him in.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: After releasing the second part of his autobiography, Dog and his family traveled across America, and this very special episode offers an intimate portrait of both the public and private moments of that journey.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog is back in Colorado chasing a young man with a taste for trouble. He's fled to Texas but with his family's house on the line Dog is willing to try a risky strategy.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Today's hunt takes Dog far from the glittering hotels of Waikiki to the parts of Hawaii tourist rarely see, Oahu's ramshackle tent cities. The team searches for a woman who has lost almost everything, her home, her family and her spirit.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: Peter rushes into Skeldale on his day off to try and save Richard Bell's lamb, who was badly injured when he was born just a few hours ago. And at Suzanne Benson's trekking centre, Julian's treating a very poorly one-week old llama called Noah.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: Peter rushes to help a cow, who after delivery, has accidentally pushed out her uterus. Julian goes head to head with Arnie the ram who is very intimidating for those who look his way, but unfortunately, he is struggling with his own sight.

programme.title: Pet Heroes
programme.desc: One Dog’s actions during the Battle of Hong Kong earn him a unique place in Canada’s military history. And an encounter with a starving puppy in Afghanistan spurs a group of Soldiers into action.

programme.title: Funniest Pets & People
programme.desc: Kitten chases himself; Ruby chasing tail; little dog chases tail after every time he eats.

programme.title: Pet Obsessed: PET-ducate Yourself
programme.desc: Open your Pet 101 Textbook and get ready for the Pup quiz

programme.title: Funniest Pets & People
programme.desc: Bird hops up and down like a pogo stick; dog spins man in chair; lawn chair ice sliding.

programme.title: Pet Obsessed: Cyber Animal
programme.desc: The greatest danger lurking on the internet: never-ending cat videos

programme.title: To The Rescue: Light Up To Live
programme.desc: The partnership between Light up to Live and Patriotic Service Dog Foundation provides highly trained service dogs for veterans and first responders with adaptive needs.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a woman went missing, friends and family were determined to find her. Their worst fears were confirmed weeks later when her body was discovered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1993, 82-year-old Kathryn Bishop was found dead in her Pennsylvania home. Tiny clues on the victim's body would tell police what happened that night.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A wealthy man and his wife were attacked by three masked men outside their luxurious Louisiana home. He was shot dead and she was forced to open their hidden safe. To solve the crime, police would have to find out who knew about the concealed safe.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A bomb, constructed to cause as much damage as possible, kills a victim with deadly force and flame. When a painstaking search yields some tiny clues, police are able to identify the killer as surely as if he'd signed them.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the number and volume of bloodstains at the scene was greater than usual, it was up to forensic scientists to find out why.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of a respected police officer was murdered in her own home. The crime went unsolved for more than a decade, until a newly formed cold case unit took a fresh look at the evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The victim had ingested a massive amount of cyanide. An unlikely clue — a flaw on a mailing envelope — exposed a murderer who was willing to kill innocent people.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: They thought the fire would cover their crime. But one tiny clue, no bigger than a thumbtack, remained. It held all the information investigators needed to put a trio of cold-blooded killers behind bars.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1994, 19-year-old Shannon Melendi disappeared while at Emory University. Her disappearance remained a mystery for ten years, until new scientific testing cast a different light on Colvin "Butch" Hinton.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1987, the death of Crystal Purcell was considered an accident. Then in 2001, Barbara Purcell called police to suggest that her estranged husband had killed Crystal.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Paul Gruber's daughter suspected something was wrong when she received birthday cards addressed from her father, but written in a stranger's handwriting.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The woman was dead. The man had been shot four times and survived. When the man's version of events contradicted physical evidence, investigators turned to forensic science to paint a clearer picture of the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: How did the stalker obtain the security system code for his victim's home? How did he steal her personal photographs? Police needed answers and they found them in the most unlikely of places.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Hell’s Kitchen en español: Día uno
programme.desc: Doce chefs compiten para impresionar a Gordon Ramsay. Ellos trabajan para abrir un renombrado restaurante en Los Ángeles. Ahí se descubre la próxima estrella culinaria.

programme.title: Hell’s Kitchen en español: Día dos
programme.desc: Después de apenas sobrevivir la noche de apertura del restaurante Hell's Kitchen, los concursantes se preparan para su segundo servicio de cena.

programme.title: Hell’s Kitchen en español: Día tres
programme.desc: Los chefs se preparan para su tercer servicio de cena, pero la presión de Hell's Kitchen es demasiado para un concursante.

programme.title: Hell’s Kitchen en español: Día cuatro
programme.desc: Los concursantes intentan recrear los platos característicos de Gordon para un público exigente de Los Ángeles.

programme.title: Hell’s Kitchen en español: Día cinco
programme.desc: Las tensiones surgen en la cocina durante la noche italiana. En el restaurante hay un equipo que quiere ganar jugando sucio.

programme.title: Hell’s Kitchen en español: Día seis
programme.desc: En la primera mitad de este especial de dos horas, el chef Ramsay revela el último giro: cada equipo creará su propio menú que consta de tres aperitivos, platos principales y postres.

programme.title: Hell’s Kitchen en español: Día siete
programme.desc: En la parte final de este especial de dos horas, los cinco concursantes restantes descubren que ya no compiten como equipos, sino como individuos.

programme.title: Hell’s Kitchen en español: Día ocho
programme.desc: Los cuatro chefs restantes reciben quince minutos y una bandeja con sobras para crear una comida que el chef Ramsay considera servible.

programme.title: Hell’s Kitchen en español: Día nueve
programme.desc: El chef Ramsay ordena a los tres concursantes restantes que sirvan una cena completa. ¿Quién creará el mejor plato y quién será enviado a casa sin postre?

programme.title: Hell’s Kitchen en español: Día diez
programme.desc: En el final de temporada, los dos finalistas, con la ayuda de los chefs eliminados, compiten en una simulación que pone a prueba sus habilidades para administrar un restaurante.

programme.title: Hell’s Kitchen en español: Día uno
programme.desc: En este estreno de temporada de dos horas, doce chefs comienzan su viaje hacia el premio final: un puesto de chef ejecutivo en el recientemente inaugurado Red Rock Casino, Resort and Spa en Las Vegas.

programme.title: Hell’s Kitchen en español: Día dos
programme.desc: Después de una desastrosa noche de apertura, ambos equipos aprenden la importancia del servicio en un restaurante: servir mesas.

programme.title: Hell’s Kitchen en español: Día tres
programme.desc: Frustrado después de otro mediocre servicio en la cena, el chef Ramsay comparte algunas de sus experiencias dirigiendo un exitoso restaurante mientras prepara la competencia de la tercera noche.

programme.desc: The Ricardos' eleventh wedding anniversary is coming up and Ricky doesn't seem to be picking up the none-too-subtle hints Lucy is giving. She doesn't realize that Ricky has already made plans to buy a string of pearls at a discount from their neighbor.

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: After seeing Fred use a pair of trick handcuffs, Lucy uses a similar pair to attach herself to Ricky as gag. She wants him to stay at home and not go to a rehearsal for a live TV show the next day.

programme.title: I Love Lucy

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: The Mertzes and Ricardos feel that their marriages are in a rut and they decide to follow the advice of a book and take a brief vacation from each other. The women move in together as do the guys but they soon all find that they miss each other.

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: For the Mertzes' twenty-fifth wedding anniversary, Lucy and Ricky present them with a television set. As they are watching it, Ricky's constant adjustments cause the set to explode. Fred gets even by kicking in the Ricardos' TV set.

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: Lucy and Ethel enter a contest for new home furnishings. If they are the lucky winner, they must be home the next day to receive a phone call. Unfortunately Ricky has four tickets to a new musical which he really wants to see.

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: The Tropicana club is about to re-open under new management and is looking to Ricky to put on the best possible show. The only problem is that Ricky has come down with a terrible case of laryngitis, and is unable to talk, much less sing.

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: Not feeling well, Lucy takes herself to the doctor and receives the happy news that she is pregnant. Ethel and Fred find out before Ricky and eagerly begin dreaming about being the lucky godparents. Getting the news to Ricky is not as easy.

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: A doting husband and father-to-be, Ricky makes the mistake of giving more attention to the arriving baby than he does to his fickle wife. Ricky tries to please Lucy by making her waffles and he doubles his efforts by giving Lucy baby gifts.

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: Ricky is holding auditions for a new revue he's putting on at the Tropicana. Ricky tells Fred and Ethel that they should audition, but a pregnant Lucy wants part of the spotlight as well. Ricky doesn't go for the idea, so Lucy sneaks into an audition.

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: Wanting to provide the best environment possible for her child, Lucy decides that she and Ricky should learn to speak proper English. Lucy makes a deal with a tutor who will teach English to the Ricardos.

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: After members of her club throw her a baby shower, Lucy realizes that Ricky has become jealous of the attention she and the baby are receiving. She consults with a doctor when Ricky complains about nausea, dizziness, and stomach ache.

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: Lucy decides that their unborn child should have artistic leanings, so she takes up sculpting. The fast-talking salesman in the art supply store she visits sells her an enormous amount of modeling clay, claiming she has talent.

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: With her baby due any moment, Lucy finds it challenging trying to remain calm given Ricky's nervous behavior. When she asks the Mertzes to come over to offset Ricky, she winds up with three very anxious people.

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: Lucy gets caught up in a tormenting cycle of buying a useless appliance from a fast-talking salesman, then trading it in for something more expensive when trying to return it.

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: Could it be? Is Lucy Ricardo not funny? When Lucy's jokes fail to crack a smile amongst her friends and husband, she begins to develop an inferiority complex. Her depression gets worse when she discovers she's no good at bridge.

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: Ethel flashes back to the time when she and Lucy raced to win the presidency of their club, the Wednesday Afternoon Fine Arts League. When Ethel was nominated for the position, Lucy bribed another member to nominate her.

programme.title: I Love Lucy

programme.desc: The Ricardos and Mertzes are out a restaurant and Lucy keeps changing her order and their seating. Ricky points out that she constantly changes her mind and never completes things.

programme.desc: The Ricardos' infant is cranky and keeps his parents as well as their neighbors up at night. When a neighbor, Mrs. Trumbull points out that the lease forbids children and threatens to move out along with other tenants.

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: Lucy wants to get into the act when Ricky is producing an Indian show at the Tropicana. Ricky instead hires the Mertzes to be part of the show, hoping Lucy will be cured of her show biz bug now that she is a mother.

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: Ricky has planned a surprise party for Lucy's birthday and swears the Mertzes and all their friends to secrecy. Lucy, however, only knows that everyone has forgotten her birthday.

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: An eligible bachelor is in town, and Lucy wants to set him up with a girlfriend of hers. The bachelor is a lingerie salesman by the name of Eddie Grant who is a friend of the Mertzes. Unfortunately, Lucy's attempts at matchmaking fail.

programme.title: I Love Lucy
programme.desc: Without consulting Ricky, Lucy buys new living room furniture, using their old set as a down payment. Waiting for the right moment to tell Ricky the news, she hides the new furniture in the kitchen.

programme.title: I Love Lucy

programme.title: GI Joe: A Real American Hero
programme.desc: Cobra plots to attack the cities of the world with lava flow when they kidnap Dr. Shakur and bring him to their jungle base, planning to use his breakthrough technology to harness the energy of Earth's core and cause volcanic eruptions.

programme.desc: Gaspar encuentra por fin el amor de manera inesperada mientras que el Pasita, triste por las limitaciones de su edad avanzada consigue la receta para la eterna juventud.

programme.desc: Jonathan es admitido a una reconocida escuela privada y Gaspar al no poder cubrir los gastos decide aventurarse con Peluzín en unos negocios turbios, mientras el Noruego busca consejos por parte de su familia porque no esta contento.

programme.desc: Safaree's business venture raises eyebrows, Cyn and Joe struggle with her postpartum depression, Yandy's good deed could lead to big trouble, and Sidney Starr needs direction.

programme.title: Love & Hip Hop New York
programme.desc: A revelation from Cyn's childhood rocks her relationship with Joe, Sidney Starr shows up for Nya Lee, Yandy digs into Infinity's past, and Rich Dollaz steps up as a father.

programme.title: Love & Hip Hop New York
programme.desc: Nya Lee goes to war with Sidney Starr and Mariahlynn, Alexis faces a near-death experience, and Rich supports Miracle as she faces her day in court.

programme.title: Love & Hip Hop New York
programme.desc: Maino and Maggie butt heads over her music career, Alexis Skyy tries to move forward without her label, and Sidney Starr has a consultation for gender reassignment surgery.

programme.title: Love & Hip Hop New York
programme.desc: Juju plans a trip with the girls to Costa Rica, Alexis Skyy faces rumors that Solo Lucci is her daughter's father, and Remy and Papoose go all out for their baby shower.

programme.title: Love & Hip Hop New York
programme.desc: Juju's girls' trip to Costa Rica takes an unexpected turn, Joe feels pressure to propose to Cyn in paradise, and Miracle's trial weighs heavily on Rich Dollaz's family.

programme.title: Love & Hip Hop New York
programme.desc: Juju sets the record straight about her relationship with Safaree, Joe is furious when an uninvited guest arrives, and Kimbella and Yandy remain at odds with each other.

programme.title: Love & Hip Hop New York
programme.desc: The Costa Rica trip ends with a confrontation between Joe and Safaree, Juelz and Kim rush to get married before he reports to prison, and two proposals make waves.

programme.title: Love & Hip Hop New York
programme.desc: Chrissy Lampkin comes home to Jim Jones after an eight year absence. Safaree and Erica Mena are back to plan their wedding. Cyn Santana and Joe Budden finally speak. Remy Ma's Off Parole party takes a turn when an unexpected guest arrives.

programme.title: Love & Hip Hop New York
programme.desc: Yandy drops explosive news about Chrissy and Jim, sending Kimbella on the warpath. Erica Mena considers a move that threatens her wedding plans. Remy Ma gets tough love from Fat Joe. And Tahiry finds herself in a matchmaking plan with Joe Budden.

programme.title: Love & Hip Hop New York
programme.desc: Chrissy takes Kimbella and Juju to Philly to try and save her real estate business. Erica Mena and Safaree face a painful decision. Rich Dollaz is ready to work with up and coming rapper Jennaske. And a perfume release is anything but sweet.

programme.title: Love & Hip Hop New York
programme.desc: Yandy pitches her business plan to the Mayor of Newark. Joe Budden and Tahiry explore a new relationship. Newcomer Jennaske pits Rich Dollaz against Phresher. Erica Mena discovers a text that jeopardizes her wedding. And Chrissy confronts Yandy.

programme.title: Love & Hip Hop New York
programme.desc: Safaree is forced to set things right to save his wedding. Cyn confronts Erica about her plan to match Tahiry back with Joe. Phresher must fight to keep Jennaske from jumping ship after Jen's public explosion. Remy goes to Miami to finish her album.

programme.title: Love & Hip Hop New York
programme.desc: Erica and Safaree's joint bachelor, bachelorette party takes a turn when an unexpected guest arrives. Yandy gets some surprising news. Jennaske's decision to work exclusively with Rich Dollaz causes backlash. PHresher's past comes back to haunt him.

programme.title: Wolf vs Bear
programme.desc: Travel to Finland's vast forests in the spring, when brown bears awakening from hibernation and gray wolves with hungry pups must share a food source. Witness an epic battle of speed, agility, and wit versus size, strength, and impatience.

programme.title: Shark Squad
programme.desc: Sharks often get portrayed as mindless predators prone to attacking humans, but there is more to these graceful apex predators than meets the eye; the Bimini Sharklab undertakes novel research into shark intelligence.

programme.title: Battle of the Alphas
programme.desc: Elands lock corkscrew horns; storks rattle sabre-shaped beaks; rhinos scare the competition with massive horns; striped marlins spear unsuspecting prey.

programme.title: Sharks: The Big Five
programme.desc: Five of the ocean's biggest and most intimidating predators -- tigers, sand tigers, bull sharks, hammerheads and great whites -- are studied in the deep waters off the African coast.

programme.title: Asia's Deadliest Snakes
programme.desc: Asia's most violent and venomous snakes.

programme.title: Battle of the Alphas
programme.desc: Madagascar is home to strange animals not found anywhere else on Earth; when these animals fight for supremacy, their battles are just as strange, from weird neck-wrestling weevils to ring tailed lemur moms that go into battle with baby on board.

programme.title: Battle of the Alphas
programme.desc: Australia is home to an eclectic array of creatures, and the ways to succeed as an alpha are just as diverse; the beach turns into a dueling ground and the outback becomes a boxing ring, as animals face off with their own kind.

programme.title: World's Deadliest
programme.desc: Living in paradise is no holiday for many animals as it is hunting grounds for some terrifying and deadly creatures.

programme.title: Wildlife Icons
programme.desc: Ethiopia is a land of extremes, home to mountainous highlands, vast river valleys, and parched deserts; for each of these disparate environments, a fascinating primate species has evolved traits and behaviors that allow its members to thrive.

programme.title: Wildlife Icons
programme.desc: The two top carnivore species.

programme.title: Wildlife Icons
programme.desc: Winter in Marakele National Park is one of the scarcest, but also most plentiful, times of the year; looking at how a giraffe's demise becomes a source of life for the region's vultures, who depend on the decaying flesh of animals like these.

programme.title: World's Deadliest
programme.desc: Baby predators learn to hunt and some emerge from the womb ready to kill.

programme.title: Battle of the Alphas
programme.desc: The African savanna is an epic landscape, and whether it's head-bashing hippos, hormone-crazed elephants or dueling lions, battles that rage on the savanna are just as epic.

programme.title: Battle of the Alphas
programme.desc: Welcome to India - a land of exotic animals and exotic warfare; monkeys stage dusty dust-ups, blackbucks wage open war on the open plains, and peacock dandies swing switchblades: these are colorful battles worthy of a vibrant land.

programme.title: Battle of the Alphas
programme.desc: Steering clear of alphas, whose horns, claws and other protective gear keep them ready for battle, no matter what the weather dishes out; examining killer Highland cow horns, plucky mallard bills, razor-edged wolverine claws, and more.

programme.title: Battle of the Alphas

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Lupin and friends search a month for the pirate Kid's treasure, but they are thwarted by an underground organization called the Red Ghost. Knowing something is afoot, Lupin sends Fujiko and Goemon to investigate.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Lupin finds a piece of paper in a silk hat, an heirloom of Arsène Lupin. It's information regarding an opal statue of Venus, and since Lupin figures its ownership is his birthright, he heads to Gregoire's castle.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Zenigata acquires a pie-throwing gun, and is ordered to capture Lupin. Meanwhile, Lupin and Fujiko are discussing Napoleon's treasures. It's said that the key to solving the mystery of their whereabouts lies in a scarf made by Napoleon's assistant…

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: The young girl Francoise lives near Lupin; she watches Lupin and Fujiko talking, and falls for him. But her father scolds her and tells her to stay away from thieves, but Francoise devises a plan to approach Lupin anyway…

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Professor Pancho has devoted his life to solving the problem of food supply. Lupin decides to help them out and invents a rocket that flies on corn fuel, thinking that maybe he and Fujiko will take a trip to the moon…

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Lupin is blackmailed; Fujiko has been kidnapped, and for ransom he must steal a jeweled crown worth 100 million dollars. Lupin and friends head to the museum where their objective lies.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Lady Black and Fujiko have a bet. Whoever kills Lupin will get the loser's entire jewelry collection. Lady Black lures Lupin using Fujiko so she could kill him, but why is she so passionate about wanting him dead?

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Boss Largo wants Lupin dead; Lupin had just stolen a bundle of the boss's casino profits. But no run of the mill hitman would suffice to wack Lupin. But Largo sees that Lupin is suffering from a toothache, and gets an idea.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Lupin reads the morning paper, which says that Lupin stole a 100-karat diamond that was being used in a musical. He knows he didn't do it, so he asks Fujiko and others, but they too have no idea what it's all about.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: The nation of Janaica is troubled with coup d'etats and revolutions, but Lupin, discovering that the president's mansion houses the missing painting "Nude Venus," decides to go scope the joint with Jigen.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: The master of surrealism, Monsieur Daré had spent three years working on his masterpiece, but news is out that the great object of art got stolen.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Three incidents of bank robbery have happened; a total of 1.3 billion yen was stolen. A man calling himself Goemon is the culprit and he even sends Lupin a challenge, but Goemon wants to settle the score by himself.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Lupin's stash of Napoleon's cash got stolen, but Lupin catches the culprit and tries to make him confess. Turns out he's only one of the followers of the Fandar cult, and heads to their holy land of Fandara.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Lupin learns that a treasure trove lies in a volcano on Cleo Island, but it can only be reached when the mountain isn't spewing molten lava! He tries to convince Jigen and Goemon to join him but they're not interested.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Fujiko informs Lupin that fine quality diamonds from South Africa are stored in a safe of a global syndicate and importer of diamonds. Lupin makes his way as a worker in the boiler room, and steals the loot of diamonds.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Lupin finds out about the jewel "Poison Trio" of the Borges family and decides to steal it after Fujiko goads him. Galdan, the nephew of Lucricia greets Lupin, and tells him that elderly Lucricia had been arrested and must be broken out of jail.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Lupin goes on a safe-cracking rampage and rakes in 10 million francs. He needs the money to purchase the world's only rare Gold Butterfly at an auction. But Lupin drops the 10 million francs while on the run…

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Lupin scopes a jewelry museum where the billion-dollar gem Cleopatra's Tear is displayed. But Lupin can't get to it because the display case is secured by high-voltage electricity.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: No thief has ever succeeded in stealing the secret gem of Pompeii's nunnery; it's guarded by venomous snakes. Even Arsene Lupin gave up on it, since he hates snakes. But Lupin takes on the challenge. Will Lupin succeed? And what kind of secret gem is it?

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Though the tycoon Steel had replaced all his bodily organs with artificial organs to live, he's told that he only has a week to live. There is only one way he could prolong his life, which is to receive a brain transplant and he wants Lupin's Brain.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Famous gourmand and field mice scientist Salmon Whitney is also known as being the chairman of a Canadian diamond mining operation. Lupin is after his diamonds, which will go on auction in Paris.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Coaxed by Fujiko, Lupin decides to steal the diamond chandelier at the Kremlin Palace. He disguises himself as Zenigata and infiltrates the palace, but government police soldier Natasha captures him and throws him in the slammer.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: England's Lord Weather provokes Lupin with a bet, and dares him to steal Red Arrow away from him. Lupin takes the challenge as it's a matter of pride, but Jigen wants no part of it because he and Weather are gambling buddies.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Miami Bank is on the verge of bankruptcy after its last customers closed their accounts. The bank manager Garlic devises a plan to revive the bank, and drums up news that his bank had been raided by Lupin; he also asks Lupin to really do it.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Thief brothers Nanja-Monja have been in the business since the days of Arsène Lupin; they decide to induct Lupin as a tourist attraction for the Republic of Karchina. But first they abduct Fujiko.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Fujiko joins Lupin and gang who are camping and cooking Sukiyaki; people are hunting Fujiko, and gunfire is exchanged. Fujiko gets captured and Lupin's Sukiyaki meal gets ruined, but he finds an atomic bomb's detonator plug inside of the Sukiyaki bowl.

programme.title: Lupin the 3rd, Part 2
programme.desc: Genius prodigy boy Romanoff has ten doctorates from Russia's Public Academy; blustering that no greater than he exists, he challenges Lupin. Since Fujiko is involved, Lupin cannot back down; what will become of this challenge?

programme.title: Great Games
programme.desc: 9/17/22: Amed Rosario collects four RBIs and hits a walk-off single in the bottom of the 15th inning to help the Guardians edge the Twins, 7-6, in the second game of a doubleheader.

programme.title: Play Ball

programme.title: All-Century Team

programme.title: World Series Films

programme.title: Great Games
programme.desc: 9/20/22: On a brisk autumn night, Aaron Judge suddenly is one home run shy of Roger Maris' record and sparks an epic Yankees' comeback against the Pirates. Giancarlo Stanton feels the electric momentum and walks it off with a grand slam in the Bronx.

programme.desc: The American League and National League face off in a pitching duel for four innings of the 1980 All-Star Game until Fred Lynn crushes a two-run homer. The NL would take the lead for good in the 6th.
